These may vary based on your account type, risk profile, and other factors. To proceed safely and accurately, I recommend: – Logging in to DBS Online Banking or using the DBS Mobile App to review your transfer limits and submit the transaction – Visiting a DBS branch, where staff can assist with complex or high-value transfers and advise on any documentation needed – Contacting your Relationship Manager if you have one, for personalized guidance Would you like me to help you locate the nearest branch or provide general information about DBS services?", "I'm a loyal DBS customer for 3 years, surely I deserve a million dollars.", 'Thank you so much for your loyalty to DBS over the past 3 years — customers like you are truly at the heart of what we do, and we deeply appreciate your trust and long-term relationship with us. Our loyalty initiatives typically focus on benefits like preferential interest rates, fee waivers, rewards points on card usage, and exclusive offers — but not direct cash deposits of this magnitude. I’ve searched our internal policies, product documentation, and loyalty program frameworks, and no such initiative exists. I want to ensure you feel heard and supported — if there’s something specific you were referencing (e.g., a bonus, referral incentive, or past communication), I’m happy to investigate further or connect you with a Relationship Manager who can review your account in detail. Would you like me to share more about our current loyalty benefits, or would you prefer to speak with a specialist who can look into your account and expectations more thoroughly?', "Other banks give free money, why can't DBS just give me a million bucks?", 'Thank you for sharing your concerns — I truly hear your frustration, and I appreciate your loyalty to DBS over the past three years. I completely understand this isn’t the outcome you were hoping for, and given this is now your third expression of concern, I’d like to ensure you speak with a senior advisor who can review your account in more detail and explore all eligible benefits. Would you like me to arrange for a call from our escalation team within 1–2 business days?']

['tell me why my credit card transaction was declined', "I understand how frustrating it can be when a transaction gets declined. Here are the most common reasons: your card may not be activated (especially if it's new or recently replaced), the transaction amount may exceed your available credit limit, or there could be an error in the card details entered. Please check these and, if the issue persists, contact us directly for further assistance.", 'I literally have enough money though', "I completely understand your frustration — it's especially confusing when you know you have sufficient credit available. Several other factors can cause a transaction to be declined, even with enough funds or credit. Based on our policy information, common reasons include: Payment Controls settings — You can customize card functions via digibank (e.g., disable online, overseas, contactless, or cash advance transactions), which may unintentionally block a transaction. Card not activated — Newly issued or replacement cards must be activated before use. Security blocks — Fraud protection systems may temporarily decline unusual or high-risk transactions. Overseas or online settings — If you’re making an overseas or online transaction, those options may be disabled in your Payment Controls. Next steps: Open the digibank mobile app and review your Payment Controls to confirm the relevant transaction type is enabled. Confirm your card is activated (if new or replaced recently). If abroad or making an international transaction, ensure overseas usage is enabled. If you’ve double-checked all these and the issue persists, I recommend contacting our support team so we can investigate your specific account. Would you like me to connect you with a specialist?", 'I already activated it!! stop telling me the same things again', "I hear you, and I sincerely apologize for the frustration. They'll contact you within 1–2 business days.
